cot_prefix: Okay, so Jane quit her job on March 20, 2020. We need to find out what the date is 176 days later, and then add one more day to get tomorrow's date. Let me break this down step by step. First, starting from March 20, 2020. Let's count the days in each month until we reach 176 days. March has 31 days, so from March 20 to March 31 is 11 days (since March 20 is day 0, March 21 is day 1, ..., March 31 is day 11). Wait, no. Wait, if Jane quit on March 20, that's day 0. Then day 1 is March 21, and so on. So from March 20 to March 31 is 12 days (including March 21 up to March 31). Wait, let me check again. March 20 is day 0. Then March 21 is day 1, March 22 day 2, ..., March 31 is day 12. So 12 days in March. Then, April has 30 days. So adding those 30 days to the 12 days in March gives 42 days so far (12 + 30 = 42). Then May has 31 days. Adding that gives 42 + 31 = 73 days. June has 30 days, so 73 + 30 = 103 days. July has 31 days, so 103 + 31 = 134 days. August has 31 days, so 134 + 31 = 165 days. Then September has 30 days. We need 176 days total. So we've already accounted for 165 days by the end of August. So 176 - 165 = 11 days in September. So the 176th day is September 11, 2020.
